title: Cutting board conditioning oil
subtitle: A food-safe mineral oil and beeswax blend for conditioning and sealing wooden cutting boards
excerpt: Wooden cutting boards and butcher's blocks need regular conditioning with a food-safe oil to prevent the wood from drying, cracking, and absorbing odours. This blend of food-grade mineral oil and beeswax applies as a liquid, sets to a light wax finish, and is safe on boards that contact food directly.
sourceNotes: Food-safe mineral oil (food grade white mineral oil, USP grade): EFSA assessment of mineral oils in food contact materials (2018). Beeswax as a wood sealing agent: standard woodworking and kitchenware reference. Never use cooking oils (olive, vegetable, linseed) on wooden cutting boards for food contact surfaces; they oxidise, turn rancid inside the wood, and are a food safety risk.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. A 100 ml jar treats a standard cutting board roughly 6 to 8 times.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps indefinitely. Make a batch and use as needed.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keeps three or more years stored at room temperature in a sealed jar. Mineral oil does not go rancid. Discard if the mixture smells unusual or develops discolouration.

content.text: A wooden cutting board that is left dry splits, warps, and develops deep cracks that trap bacteria. Regular conditioning with a food-safe oil keeps the wood fibres flexible and seals the surface against moisture and food residues. This blend uses food-grade mineral oil as the penetrating base and beeswax to seal the surface and give a light protective finish. Never use olive oil, coconut oil, or other culinary oils on wooden boards that contact food; they go rancid inside the wood within weeks.

items.prepNote: USP food-grade mineral oil only; available from pharmacies and online
food-grade; in pellets or grated from a block

content.text: Melt the beeswax over a water bath to around 75 degrees C. Add the mineral oil and stir to combine. The mineral oil is already liquid; it cools the wax slightly when added. Stir until the mixture is uniform.

Pour into a clean wide-mouthed jar while still liquid. Leave to set at room temperature for 1 hour. The finished consistency is softer than a pure beeswax product and scoops easily with a finger or cloth.
How to condition a cutting board

Wash and thoroughly dry the board before conditioning. Apply the oil blend generously with a clean cloth or kitchen paper, working in the direction of the wood grain. Leave for at least 4 hours, or overnight. Wipe off the excess with a clean cloth. For a new or very dry board, apply three coats over three days before using. For a board in regular use, condition monthly.
Store in a sealed jar at room temperature. Keeps indefinitely; mineral oil does not oxidise or go rancid. Discard only if contaminated or if the jar develops an unusual smell.
